text:
Jonny and Alistair Brownlee, two of Britain's most decorated triathletes, will not be representing Team GB at the Paris Olympics this year. The Yorkshire-born brothers will be absent from the 2024 Games in France, each due to different circumstances.

Alistair, a two-time Olympic gold medallist, chose to retire from racing at Olympic distance after missing the Tokyo Games three years ago because of a persistent ankle injury.

Alistair underwent surgery on his ankle in 2015, but it started troubling him again in March 2021, necessitating further surgery to remove scar tissue around his tendons. Alex Yee and Jonny represented Team GB at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021, with Yee securing a silver medal and Jonny finishing fifth.

The competition was ultimately won by Norway's Kristian Blummenfelt, while New Zealand's Hayden Wilde took the bronze. Now aged 36, Alistair has retired from Olympic Triathlon boasting a gold medal from both the London 2012 and Rio 2016 Olympics, as well as two championship titles from the 2014 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow in the triathlon and mixed-team relay events.

In a shocking turn of events for British triathlon, Olympic hero Jonny Brownlee has been overlooked for the Paris 2024 squad in favour of up-and-coming athlete Sam Dickinson, despite Brownlee's stellar Olympic pedigree. At 34, the decorated triathlete - who boasts an impressive collection of gold, silver, and bronze medals from previous Games - was surpassed by Sam Dickinson who outperformed him at a key event in June.



Jonny Brownlee, disheartened by the decision, took toInstagramto express his feelings: "I have not been selected for the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. The Tokyo mixed team relay was one of the many highlights of my career and I still believe I could have helped Team GB win another medal in Paris."

He continued to show his undying support for Team GB: "I will be supporting the Team in whatever way I can and really hope they go on to defend the title! I have achieved more than I could ever have imagined in my Olympic career. After 99 world triathlon starts, 52 podiums and 3 Olympic medals, it's not the fairytale ending that I'd wished for, but Paris was always a bonus, and it can be the unwritten chapter of an incredible book! I have a great summer to look forward to and some exciting racing ahead."

The younger Brownlee sibling captured the nation's heart with top finishes across three Olympic Games, including a memorable gold in Tokyo 2021's mixed relay event, partnering with Jessica Learmonth, Georgia Taylor-Brown, and Alex Yee to clinch victory in a formidable time of 1:23:41. Beyond his recent gold, Brownlee also took home the silver medal in Rios 2016 triathlon and snagged a bronze in London 2012, asserting his status as a true British sporting icon.

Taylor-Brown, Dickinson and Kate Waugh will be flying the flag for Team GB in the finals in Paris, joined by Yee and Beth Potter. The men's final is scheduled for 7am today (July 30), with the women's final set to follow on Wednesday (July 31) at the same time.

title: Why are Alistair and Jonny Brownless not competing at Olympics 2024? | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Aaron Morris
description: Alistair and Jonny Brownlee have been two of the most successful triathletes in Team GB history, but neither will be competing at the Paris Olympics this summer
